basic_details.job_title: BITS Pilani Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for Junior Research Fellow /Project Associate I Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Birla Institute of Techniology and Science Pilani (BITS Pilani)
basic_details.short_information: Birla Institute of Technology and Science Pilani (BITS Pilani) Junior Research Fellow /Project Associate I Notification 2026 has been released on 09 May 2026 on the official website bits-pilani.ac.in. A total of 1 vacancy has been announced through the BITS Pilani Junior Research Fellow /Project Associate I Recruitment 2026. Candidates up to 28 years of age who have M.S./M.Sc./M.Tech. or equivalent degree in Physics or related disciplines are eligible to apply for these Junior Research Fellow /Project Associate I vacancies. The email application process for BITS Pilani Junior Research Fellow /

age_limit.details: Maximum Age Limit: 28 years Age relaxation will be provided as per government rules.

qualification.details: M.S./M.Sc./M.Tech. or equivalent degree in Physics or related disciplines with a minimum of 60% marks (or equivalent CGPA). Preference to NET/GATE qualified candidates. Prior experience in Computational Physics and Machine Learning is preferred. Basic knowledge of MATLAB or Python is desirable.

how_to_apply: Read the official notification carefully before applying.
Prepare your application with complete bio-data including personal details, educational qualifications, and experience.
Attach self-attested copies of all required documents such as educational certificates, experience certificates, ID proof, and recent passport-size photographs.
Clearly mention the post applied for in the subject line of the email.
Send the complete application to the official email address mentioned in the notification.
Ensure the email is sent before the last date: 20-06-2026.
Keep a copy of the sent email for future reference.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for the BITS Pilani Junior Research Fellow (JRF)/Project Associate-I recruitment?
faq.answer: The last date to submit your application via email is 20th June 2026. Ensure your application reaches the concerned authority before this deadline.

faq.question: Q2. What are the minimum educational qualifications required for this post?
faq.answer: Candidates must possess an M.S./M.Sc./M.Tech. or an equivalent degree in Physics or related disciplines with at least 60% marks.

faq.question: Q3. Is there any preference for candidates who have qualified in NET/GATE?
faq.answer: Yes, candidates who have qualified in NET/GATE or other national-level examinations are preferred for this position.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for the Junior Research Fellow (JRF)/Project Associate-I position?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit for this post is 28 years. Age relaxation will be provided as per government rules.

faq.question: Q5. What is the salary offered for the Junior Research Fellow (JRF)/Project Associate-I role?
faq.answer: The selected candidates will receive a monthly salary ranging from Rs. 30,000/- to Rs. 37,000/-.

faq.question: Q6. How will candidates be selected for this recruitment?
faq.answer: The selection process involves shortlisting candidates based on merit, experience, and project requirements, followed by an online interview.

faq.question: Q7. Is there any application fee for BITS Pilani recruitment?
faq.answer: No, there is no application fee mentioned for any category (General/OBC, SC/ST) for this recruitment.

faq.question: Q8. What is the mode of application for this recruitment?
faq.answer: The application process is to be done via email. Candidates need to send their complete application along with required documents to the specified email address.

faq.question: Q9. Does BITS Pilani offer any age relaxation for reserved categories?
faq.answer: Yes, age relaxation will be provided to candidates belonging to reserved categories as per the prevailing government rules and regulations.

faq.question: Q10. What kind of experience is preferred for this role?
faq.answer: Research experience in Computational Physics and Machine Learning is preferred for candidates applying for the Junior Research Fellow (JRF)/Project Associate-I position.
